living-concrete.knowledge:
Books are LIVING CONTENT. Like packages have living-abstract, books have PURPOSE.
PURPOSE drives: scope, audience, depth, writing style.
Define PURPOSE FIRST (in README.md): - Essence: What IS this book? - Purpose: WHY does it exist? - Audience: WHO is reading? - Scope: What's IN/OUT?
PURPOSE-first prevents: scope creep, audience confusion, style inconsistency.

README.md ≠ index.md (separation of concerns)
| File | Role | Part of Book? | |------|------|---------------| | README.md | Book's living-abstract (meta) | NO | | index.md | Table of Contents (content root) | YES |
README.md contains: purpose, audience, scope, status, corpus membership index.md contains: chapter list, navigation, reading entry point
Pocket edition generates from index.md down, not README.md.

Traditional book structure beyond chapters:
FRONT MATTER (before chapter 1): | Element | Purpose | |---------|---------| | Jacket | The pitch/hook (marketing, "why read this") | | Foreword | Third-party introduction/endorsement | | Preface | Author's context, why this book exists | | Introduction | How to read, prerequisites, orientation | | Prologue | Narrative scene-setting before Ch1 |
BACK MATTER (after last chapter): | Element | Purpose | |---------|---------| | Epilogue | Reflection, where to go next | | Appendices | Supplementary deep-dives | | Glossary | Terminology reference | | Bibliography | Sources, further reading | | About Authors | Credibility, perspective | | Index | Navigation aid |
Not all books need all elements. Use what serves the PURPOSE.

Books exist in CONTEXT, not isolation.
DECLARATION (in README.md): ```yaml part-of: BentOS Bible corpus-link: ../index.md position: 3 # reading order prerequisites: [ontology, physical-sciences] ```
SERIES CONTINUITY (in prose): Books in a corpus are like TV series episodes — they weave connections narratively.
| Technique | Purpose | |-----------|---------| | Recap | "Previously..." summary at book/chapter start | | Callbacks | Reference earlier books inline ("As we saw in...") | | Foreshadowing | Hint at upcoming books | | Shared vocabulary | Terms defined once, used across corpus |
Context is STORYTELLING, not just metadata. The narrative weaves the connections.

STATUS = lifecycle stage with semantic meaning.
| Status | Optimized For | Appropriate Work | |--------|---------------|------------------| | DRAFT | Fast iteration | Structure, content, rough OK | | REVIEW | Editorial polish | Style, clarity, consistency | | RC | Final checks | Fixes only, no new content | | PUBLISHED | Stability | Changes = new edition | | DEPRECATED | End-of-life | Banner + successor link | | ARCHIVED | Historical | Removed from TOC |
Directory with ordered markdown = BookEntity Patterns: numbered (01-*.md), named (chapter-*.md), logical sequence Special files: README.md (meta), index.md (TOC), front matter, back matter

Apps are atoms. Style comes from bonded skills.
books.app attracts craft/writing/* → style from writing skill books.app attracts discipline/synthesizing/* → cognitive approach
Same structure, different style. Composition determines output.

Obsidian-friendly patterns delegated to bentos.tools.markdown.obsidian.skill. This app requires that skill for portable markdown output. See skill for: frontmatter schema, link patterns, tag conventions.
living-concrete.pattern: Before writing content: define PURPOSE in README.md. Before each chapter: check alignment with PURPOSE. Before advancing status: verify PURPOSE fulfilled.

Books in a corpus connect through NARRATIVE, not just links.
RECAP: Open chapters/books with brief context from prior material. CALLBACKS: "As we explored in [Book]..." woven into prose naturally. HOOKS: End chapters/books with anticipation for what's next.
Think TV series: each episode stands alone but rewards series viewers.
DRAFT → REVIEW → RC → PUBLISHED Each transition = quality gate, not just label change.

REQUIRED: 1. README.md with PURPOSE + corpus membership 2. index.md as content entry point
CHAPTERS: 3. YAML front matter (status, version) 4. Navigation block (Back/TOC/Continue) 5. Cross-links on first mention
STYLE: Delegated to bonded craft/writing/* skill.
living-concrete.protocol:
Generate compressed book for context loading. COMPRESSED, not summarized.
MANTRA: "Same journey, less words." TARGET: ~20-30% of original (NOT ~5% outline) SOURCE: index.md down (not README.md)
FIDELITY: - PRESERVE voice, chapter structure, key examples - COMPRESS repetition, elaboration, multiple examples - CUT redundancy, tangents, restatements
TEST: Reader thinks "I read the book, just faster" not "I read a summary"
living-concrete.antipattern: DON'T: Write books that ignore their corpus context DON'T: Assume reader knows project context (e.g., "what is BentOS?") DO: Declare corpus membership, use series continuity techniques DO: Recap/callback to connect with larger narrative
DON'T: Use README.md as book content entry point DO: README.md = meta (purpose), index.md = content (TOC)
DON'T: Start writing without defined PURPOSE DON'T: Let chapters drift from stated scope/audience DO: PURPOSE-first, check alignment throughout
DON'T: Enforce style elements as mandatory structure DO: Enforce structure; let bonded skill determine style
